items.title: Launch of a Cooperation Project for Women’s and Girls’ Leadership in Senegal
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 24 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Senegal, Michele Tommasi, spoke at the first meeting of the National Steering Committee for the Parel/F project—an initiative funded by Italian Cooperation to strengthen the leadership and socioeconomic inclusion of women and girls in Senegal—to reaffirm Italy’s support for this initiative. The meeting was opened by the Chief of Staff of the Ministry of Family, Social Action, and Solidarity, Amadou Diaw, and by the Director of AICS Dakar, Giovanni Grandi, officially launching the project’s operational phase. This first meeting made it possi
items.titleIt: Launch of a Cooperation Project for Women’s and Girls’ Leadership in Senegal

items.title: Africa: AU, U.S. Envoy Discuss Aussom's Future Amid Funding Uncertainty
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- The head of the African Union Support and Stabilization Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM) met the acting U.S. ambassador to Somalia in Mogadishu to discuss the mission's future as it faces growing financial uncertainty, the AU mission said.
items.titleIt: Africa: AU, U.S. Envoy Discuss Aussom's Future Amid Funding Uncertainty

items.title: Ambassador to Senegal Opens Goreé School for Sustainable Migration
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 21 LUG - Italian Ambassador to Senegal Michele Tommasi attended the opening ceremony of the Gorée School 2026, organized as part of the project “Training, Dignity, Inclusion, and Innovation in Senegal,” funded by Italian Cooperation. Organized by the Institute for the Rights of Migrant Workers, in partnership with Inca Cgil Senegal, VIS, and numerous Italian and Senegalese institutional and social actors, the Gorée School serves as a unique forum for dialogue and exchange. It brings together institutions, labor unions, development cooperation actors, civil society organizati
items.titleIt: Ambassador to Senegal Opens Goreé School for Sustainable Migration

items.title: Confindustria sounds alarm about outlook amid fresh conflict in the Gulf
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 24 - The CSC research unit of Italian industry association Confindustria on Friday sounded the alarm about the nation's economic outlook following a breakdown of the Iran war ceasefire. "The third quarter is off to a bad start," the CSC said in its latest economic outlook report. "The war in Iran flared up again in July, and uncertainty about future developments is at a peak: oil prices were immediately affected. "Having already slowed Italian GDP in the second quarter, the war's impact is now spilling over into the third,. "Inflation will remain high for some time and credi
items.titleIt: Confindustria sounds alarm about outlook amid fresh conflict in the Gulf

items.title: Italy’s SACE and Bank of Africa in Rabat Join Forces for the Mattei Plan
items.description: SACE, the Italian Export Credit Agency owned by the Ministry of Economy and Finance, and Bank of Africa, one of the leading banking groups on the African continent, have signed an agreement to invest jointly in Africa and promote the involvement of Italian companies in strategic projects being carried out on the continent. The agreement, signed by Mario Melillo on behalf of Sace and Khalid Nasr on behalf of Bank of Africa, brings together the African bank’s extensive presence and Sace’s financial and insurance solutions to support the competitiveness and internationalization of Italian compani
items.titleIt: Italy’s SACE and Bank of Africa in Rabat Join Forces for the Mattei Plan

items.title: Woman, 55, dies two months after being hit in Modena car attack
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 22 - A 55-year-old woman who was among the people hit by a car attack in the northern city of Modena on May 16 has died, the hospital she was being treated at said on Wednesday. Monica Esposito never regained consciousness after the attack by a 31-year-old Italian citizen of Moroccan origin with mental-health problems, Salim El Koudri. After driving into the pedestrians with his car, El Koudri pulled a knife when passers-by, including two Egyptian men, grappled him to ground. Esposito was among eight people injured in the attack. El Koudri is an economics graduate who had no
items.titleIt: Woman, 55, dies two months after being hit in Modena car attack

items.title: Ethiopia: U.S.-Ethiopia Public Private Dialogue to Benefit U.S. and Ethiopian Businesses and Economic Growth
items.description: [U.S. Embassy Addis Ababa] Addis Ababa -- U.S. Ambassador to Ethiopia Ervin J. Massinga co-chaired a Public-Private Dialogue (PPD) with Ethiopian Investment Commission (EIC) Commissioner Dr. Zeleke Temesgen and Chief Macroeconomic Advisor to the Prime Minister Ambassador Girma Biru at the Skylight Hotel in Addis Ababa on July 23. The dialogue focused on advancing commercial diplomacy, expanding market access, and ensuring a level playing field for U.S. companies operating in Ethiopia. By bringing together representatives from the American Chamber
items.titleIt: Ethiopia: U.S.-Ethiopia Public Private Dialogue to Benefit U.S. and Ethiopian Businesses and Economic Growth

items.title: South Sudan: Arms Smuggling - a Key Threat to South Sudan's December Elections
items.description: [ISS] South Sudan's fragile transition is increasingly shaped by forces beyond its borders that drive illicit arms flows into the country.
items.titleIt: South Sudan: Arms Smuggling - a Key Threat to South Sudan's December Elections

items.title: Sudan: 80 Sudanese Leave Uganda On Voluntary Return Flight
items.description: [Dabanga] Amsterdam / Kampala -- The 11th voluntary return flight carrying Sudanese refugees from Uganda to Sudan departed on Thursday with 80 returnees on board. The flight travelled to Port Sudan Airport aboard a Tarco Aviation aircraft and was sponsored by Sudan's General Intelligence Service.
items.titleIt: Sudan: 80 Sudanese Leave Uganda On Voluntary Return Flight
